name: Pickled Garlic
recipeIngredient: 1/3 lb Fresh ginger; peeled and thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine the garlic in a large saucepan with enough water to cover. Place over medium-high heat and bring to a boil. Let boil for 2 minutes.
2. Drain thoroughly. When cool enough to handle, peel each clove without crushing.
3. Transfer the peeled cloves to a non-metallic bowl. Add ginger and salt with enough water to cover. Refrigerate the mixture for 2 days.
4. Drain the garlic mixture and rinse thoroughly in cold water. Drain again.
5. Pack ginger and garlic evenly into 7 clean hot half-pint jars, leaving about 1/2 inch from the top.
6. Add 1 chili to each jar.
7. Combine vinegar, wine, and mustard seed in a medium saucepan and bring to a rapid boil over high heat.
8. Ladle enough hot brine to cover the garlic mixture.
9. Run a plastic knife or spatula between the mixture and jar to release any air bubbles.
10. Clean the rim and threads of the jar with a damp cloth.
11. Seal with a new, scalded, very hot lid.
12. Repeat with the remaining jars.
13. Transfer jars to gently simmering water bath (180-190 degrees) and process for 10 minutes.
14. Let cool on a rack. Check for seal.
15. Store in a cool, dry place. Makes about 6 jars.
